pattern: Since a value with frequency f must occupy f different rows to stay distinct within each row, assigning its k-th occurrence to row k-1 automatically produces the minimum possible number of rows.
hints: The number of rows can never be smaller than the largest number of times any single value repeats, because each repeat of that value needs its own row.
Track, for every distinct value, how many times you have already placed it; send each new occurrence of a value to the row matching that running count so no row ever receives the same value twice.

Walking nums once while keeping a per-value occurrence counter (opening a new row exactly when a counter reaches the current row total) reaches that minimum row count directly, with no need to plan the grouping in advance.

edge_inputs.description: one value repeats three times among others that repeat less
every value distinct, a single row suffices
single-element array, one row with one value
one value repeated across every slot, one row per occurrence
a short array with one duplicate pair, easy to over-count rows for
two different values alternate with unequal repeat counts
two distinct values tie for the maximum frequency
value equal to the array length, exercising the upper bound of the value range

stress.description: random values up to the current length so several values collide with varying frequencies
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 200))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, n) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
stress.note: values drawn from 1..n keeps frequencies within the constraint and produces a good mix of row counts
